title: When Assigning Blame: - Pause: Ask,
subtitle: Check Blame Bias
description:
When assigning blame:
- Pause: Ask, "Am I blaming more because the outcome was worse?"
- Separate outcome from intent: Focus on actions, not just consequences.
- Avoid personal filters: Check if you’re influenced by similarities to the victim.

Example: In a car accident case, avoid assigning more blame just because the damage was severe.

faq.question: How do I track my progress with this habit?
faq.answer: Use the check-in method described above and track it in the Life OS app for consistent progress monitoring.

faq.question: What if I miss a day or forget to do this habit?
faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.
